reasoning: Based on overwhelming evidence, Ukraine will not announce a presidential election before July 1, 2025. Key facts established: (1) Martial law extended until August 6, 2025 (after resolution date) with overwhelming parliamentary support (357-1 vote) on April 16, 2025; (2) Ukrainian constitution strictly prohibits elections during martial law; (3) Parliament passed resolution (286-0) explicitly stating no elections until martial law lifted and sustainable peace achieved; (4) President Zelenskyy consistently stated elections only after martial law ends; (5) Even if martial law were lifted on May 16 (knowledge cutoff), insufficient time remains for consensus-building, legal framework creation, and official announcement within the 45-day window to July 1. The military situation and political consensus strongly favor maintaining current arrangements.
